README.md:
# WooCommerce MCP Shopping Server

Generated by Adeptra Merchant for **mystore.example.com**.

## What this is

An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that lets an AI shopping agent search
this store's product catalog and build a cart, using WooCommerce's built-in
Store API. Its tools follow the same catalog/cart vocabulary your UCP manifest
declares.

## The hard boundary — read this first

**This server reads your catalog and builds carts. It never takes payment —
checkout stays on your store.** Nothing in this server calls WooCommerce's
checkout/payment endpoints or exposes admin data. The `begin_checkout` tool
returns the current cart plus your store's normal checkout URL; the shopper
(or the agent, on the shopper's behalf) completes the purchase there, through
your existing payment setup. Adeptra generated and configured this server —
it does not host it, and it never touches your money.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 18 or later, on a host you control (a VPS, a small cloud instance,
  a container — anywhere that can run a long-lived Node process). Adeptra
  never hosts this for you.
- Your store must expose the WooCommerce Store API (standard on modern
  WooCommerce/WooCommerce Blocks). Verify with:
  `curl https://yourstore.example.com/wp-json/wc/store/v1/products`
  You should get back a JSON array, not a 404 or a login page.

## Install

```bash
cd mcp-server
npm install
npm run build
```

## Configure

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Edit `.env` and set `WOOCOMMERCE_STORE_URL` to your real store URL. No API
key or secret is required — the Store API's catalog and cart endpoints are
public/unauthenticated by design (that's also why this server can't touch
payment or admin data: it was never given the keys to).

## Run

```bash
npm start
```

This starts the MCP server over stdio — the standard way MCP clients (agent
runtimes, UCP-speaking hosts) launch and talk to a local MCP server process.

## Connect it to your UCP manifest

Once this server is deployed somewhere reachable, point your UCP manifest's
`dev.ucp.shopping` service endpoint at its deployed address (replacing the
placeholder Adeptra's manifest fix left there), so agents that discover your
manifest know where to reach this shopping server.

## You must complete

- [ ] Deploy this to a host you control — Adeptra does not host it for you.
- [ ] Set the real `WOOCOMMERCE_STORE_URL` in `.env`.
- [ ] Confirm the Store API responds (see Prerequisites above).
- [ ] Point your UCP manifest's shopping service endpoint at this server's
      deployed address, then re-run your Adeptra analysis — that's what turns
      "cart/catalog/checkout capability enabled once deployed" into a genuine pass.

## Tools this server exposes

| Tool | Does |
|---|---|
| `search_products` | Search the catalog by keyword |
| `get_product` | Get one product's full details by id |
| `add_to_cart` | Add a product to the cart |
| `update_cart_item` | Change a cart line item's quantity |
| `remove_cart_item` | Remove a cart line item |
| `get_cart` | Get current cart contents + totals |
| `begin_checkout` | Return the cart + your store's checkout URL (no payment) |
